steps.title: Use the two charge points
steps.content: The answer choices are linear functions of the number of lessons, so use two given data points. From $(6,150)$ to $(17,480)$, the slope is $m=\frac{480-150}{17-6}=30.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=-30$.

steps.title: Interpret the calculation
steps.content: The slope $30$ means each additional lesson adds 30 dollars. The intercept $-30$ is not a literal fee here; it is the intercept of the simplified linear rule for $x\gt 2$. The graph still has to pass through the two real charge points, and $y=30x-30$ does.

steps.title: Write $f(x)$
steps.content: Use the fitted equation as the function: $f(x)=30x-30$. Check the two given points: $f(6)=30(6)-30=150$ and $f(17)=30(17)-30=480$.
